name: Stability and Consistency Imply Convergence

statement_latex:
\begin{lemma}
Under the conditions of Lemma~\ref{lem:515A}, the exact solution and the computed solution in a step are related by
\[
\tilde{y}_i^{[n]} - y_i^{[n]} = \sum_{j=1}^{r} V_{ij} \left( \tilde{y}_j^{[n-1]} - y_j^{[n-1]} \right) + K_i^{[n]}, \qquad i = 1, 2, \dots, r,
\]
where
\[
\|K^{[n]}\| \leq h \alpha \max_{i=1}^{r} \left| \tilde{y}_i^{[n-1]} - y_i^{[n-1]} \right| + \beta h^2,
\]
and $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are given by
\[
\alpha = L \max_{i=1}^{s} |\ell_i|,
\]
where $\ell$ is given by
\[
\sum_{j=1}^{s} (\delta_{ij} - h_0 L |a_{ij}|) \ell_j = \sum_{j=1}^{s} |U_{ij}|, \qquad i = 1, 2, \dots, s,
\]
and
\[
\beta = L^2 M \max_{i=1}^{s} \left( \frac{1}{2} |u_i| + |v_i| + \sum_{j=1}^{s} |b_{ij} c_j| + h_0 L \sum_{j=1}^{s} |b_{ij}| \ell_j \right),
\]
where $\ell$ is as in Lemma~\ref{lem:515A}.
\end{lemma}

proof_latex:
\begin{proof}
From \eqref{eq:515c}, and the relation
\[
y_i^{[n]} - h \sum_{j=1}^{s} b_{ij} f(Y_j) - \sum_{j=1}^{r} V_{ij} y_j^{[n-1]} = 0,
\]
we have
\begin{align*}
&\left| \tilde{y}_i^{[n]} - y_i^{[n]} - \sum_{j=1}^{r} V_{ij} \left( \tilde{y}_j^{[n-1]} - y_j^{[n-1]} \right) \right| \\
&\quad \leq h \sum_{j=1}^{s} |b_{ij}| \left| f(\tilde{Y}_j) - f(Y_j) \right| \\
&\qquad + h^2 L^2 M \left( \frac{1}{2} |u_i| + |v_i| + \sum_{j=1}^{s} |b_{ij} c_j| + h_0 L \sum_{j=1}^{s} |b_{ij}| \ell_j \right) \\
&\quad \leq h L \sum_{j=1}^{s} |b_{ij}| \left| \tilde{Y}_j - Y_j \right| \\
&\qquad + h^2 L^2 M \left( \frac{1}{2} |u_i| + |v_i| + \sum_{j=1}^{s} |b_{ij} c_j| + h_0 L \sum_{j=1}^{s} |b_{ij}| \ell_j \right). \tag{515d}
\end{align*}
Bound $\eta_j = \tilde{Y}_j - Y_j$ using the estimate
\[
\left| \tilde{Y}_j - Y_j - \sum_{k=1}^{r} U_{jk} \left( \tilde{y}_k^{[n-1]} - y_k^{[n-1]} \right) \right| \leq h L \sum_{k=1}^{s} |a_{jk}| \cdot \left| \tilde{Y}_k - Y_k \right|,
\]
which leads to
\[
\sum_{k=1}^{s} (\delta_{jk} - h_0 L |a_{jk}|) \eta_k \leq \sum_{k=1}^{r} |U_{jk}| \max_{k=1}^{r} \left| \tilde{y}_k^{[n-1]} - y_k^{[n-1]} \right|
\]
and to
\[
\left| \tilde{Y}_j - Y_j \right| \leq h \ell_j \max_{k=1}^{s} \left| \tilde{Y}_k - Y_k \right|.
\]
Substitute this bound into \eqref{eq:515d} and we obtain the required result.

To complete the argument that stability and consistency imply convergence, we estimate the global error in the computation of $y(x)$ by carrying out $n$ steps from an initial value $y(x_0)$ using a stepsize equal to $h = (x - x_0)/n$.
\end{proof}
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
The local truncation error for a Runge--Kutta-like method is analyzed. Let $y_i^{[n]}$ be the exact solution at stage $i$ of step $n$, and $\tilde{y}_i^{[n]}$ the computed solution. The errors are compared using a Lipschitz constant $L$ for $f$ and previous error bounds. Lemma 515B relates the errors at step $n$ to errors at step $n-1$ plus a local term $K^{[n]}$. Let $V_{ij}$ be coefficients from Lemma 515A, $h$ the step size, and $\eta_i$ quantities bounding local truncation errors. The inequality (515c) bounds $\|y_i^{[n]}-\tilde{y}_i^{[n]}\|$.

variables.name: y_i^[n]
variables.definition: exact solution at stage i of step n

variables.name: \tilde{y}_i^[n]
variables.definition: computed solution at stage i of step n

variables.name: V_{ij}
variables.definition: coefficients in the error propagation relation (from Lemma 515A)

variables.name: L
variables.definition: Lipschitz constant for the function f

variables.name: \eta_i
variables.definition: quantities defined in the preceding text, used to bound local truncation errors

equations.content: Lemma 515A (conditions referenced but not fully stated here) provides conditions on the numerical method and error propagation.
An inequality bounding the difference between exact and computed stage values, derived earlier in the subsection.

preamble: The text analyzes the local truncation error for a Runge-Kutta-like method. It involves comparing exact stage values y_i^[n] and computed stage values \tilde{y}_i^[n] over steps, using Lipschitz conditions and previous error bounds. Lemma 515B relates the errors at step n to errors at step n-1 plus a local term K^[n].
